element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player chooses from 6 playable teams (regional variants)
Turn-based pitching and batting alternation establishes core game rhythm
Speed control (km/h or mph) and pitch placement become skill expression
Timing and swing positioning for contact and power hits

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based sports rhythm with discrete tension cycles per pitch; matches consist of 9 innings with natural relief points at inning breaks

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible team selection and basic pitch/bat mechanics introduce sports fundamentals
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Gradual increase in opponent skill and pitch variety as matches progress
difficulty_curve.late_game: Late innings may feature more aggressive AI pitching and fielding strategies

notable_level_patterns.description: Each inning consists of top (player pitches) and bottom (player bats) halves; 9-inning match structure creates repeating gameplay loop with clear progression markers
Discrete action phases (pitch selection, throw speed, batter timing) create pause points between tension escalations

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Baseball NES/FDS article)
